security_gap.gap_description: The proof data required to claim incentives might be forgeable or replayable.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with the claim mechanism.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromised proof integrity allows unauthorized claiming of incentive tokens, leading to asset loss.

assumptions: The backend service generating proofs does not sufficiently secure or sign the proof data.
The BGTIncentiveDistributor contract does not fully validate the authenticity or non-replayability of the submitted proof.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic signing of claim proofs by a trusted off-chain service, and verify signatures on-chain.

description: An attacker may spoof or replay claim proof data, potentially allowing them to claim incentive tokens that belong to other users or claim tokens multiple times.

security_gap.gap_description: Proof data or associated claims may be tampered with before submission to the contract.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with the claim mechanism.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with the claim proof could redirect incentives or inflate claim amounts, leading to unauthorized asset transfer.

assumptions: The claim proof data format or validation is susceptible to modification.
The link between the proof and the specific claim amount/recipient is not fully secured.

mitigation.strategy: Hash or sign the proof data and the specific claim details together; validate the composite hash/signature on-chain.

description: An attacker may tamper with the proof data or the claim request before submitting it to the BGTIncentiveDistributor contract, potentially altering the recipient or amount of incentive tokens claimed.
